TerminosCondiciones.title: Terms and Conditions of Heinsohn Business Technology S.A. BIC

subsections.subtitle: Cloud services provider – contractor
subsections.content: Heinsohn Business Technology S.A. BIC, identified with NIT 800.046.226-8, incorporated in the city of Bogotá and registered with the Bogotá Chamber of Commerce.

subsections.subtitle: Cloud services consumer – contracting party
subsections.content: For all intents and purposes, this represents a natural or legal person who contracts the services provided by the cloud services provider – contractor, in exchange for an agreed value, maintaining a contractual relationship with the latter. They will be clearly identified in the service orders.

subsections.subtitle: Joint denomination
subsections.content: When these terms and conditions refer jointly to the cloud services provider – contractor and the cloud services consumer – contracting party, they shall be referred to as 'the parties'.

sections.title: Purpose of the terms and conditions
paragraphs: The purpose of these terms and conditions is to regulate the legal aspects of the business relationship between the parties, so that each service they agree upon is formalized through a service order.

The service orders will include the specific conditions of each business, which will constitute the contractual regulation applicable between the parties and which shall be interpreted jointly and complementarily with the provisions herein.

It is clear and accepted by the parties that their only obligations shall be those expressly included in the corresponding service order. Anything not expressly regulated in the service orders shall be governed by the provisions of these terms and conditions, which are accessory to the service order.

sections.title: Payment conditions
paragraphs: The cloud services consumer - contracting party shall pay the cloud services provider - contractor the amounts established in the respective service orders, within the timeframes and under the conditions agreed upon between the parties. The invoices generated by the service orders will be filed as indicated therein and, unless the service order indicates otherwise, the amounts will be payable thirty (30) calendar days after the invoice is filed at the address and/or email indicated by the cloud services consumer - contracting party.

The cloud services provider - contractor will present the invoices in compliance with applicable regulations. Therefore, they must include at least the following information: (i) name of the cloud services provider - contractor; (ii) physical and/or electronic address of the cloud services provider - contractor; (iii) date; (iv) payment concept and description of the services actually provided, and (v) amount to be paid.
The cloud services consumer - contracting party must provide the cloud services provider - contractor with the email and physical address where the electronic billing will be sent.

First paragraph: Any change or modification to the specified email must be communicated to the cloud services provider - contractor at the email address cartera@heinsohn.com.co. The request for change or modification will not take effect until the cloud services provider - contractor acknowledges receipt of said change or modification. Therefore, the cloud services provider - contractor will not accept the return of billing if the document is rejected due to a change of the filing email without compliance with the provisions herein.

Second paragraph: In the event of a default in the payment of any of the amounts stipulated in the corresponding service order, the cloud services consumer - contracting party shall recognize in favor of the cloud services provider - contractor default interest at the maximum market rate established by the competent national authorities for the corresponding period, together with compensation for any damages that may be caused to the cloud services provider - contractor, such as expenses or costs incurred as a result of the breach, without prejudice to any legal actions that may arise.

Third paragraph: A breach in any of the payments by the cloud services consumer - contracting party entitles the cloud services provider - contractor to, at its discretion, suspend or terminate the provision of the service, without these actions constituting a contractual breach. Therefore, the consumer cannot demand the provision of the services subject to the breached order, nor claim any compensation, penalty, or indemnification in their favor. If the provider opts to suspend the service, it must demand the due payment and notify the consequence of the breach with no less than five (5) calendar days prior to executing the suspension. If the provider opts to terminate the contract, it must demand the due payment and notify the consequence of the breach with at least fifteen (15) calendar days prior to executing the action.

Fourth paragraph: Under no circumstances will refunds be made for the amounts paid by the cloud services consumer – contracting party to the cloud services provider - contractor for services actually provided under the service orders.

Fifth paragraph: The corresponding tax amount will be added to the values stipulated in the service orders, which will be settled according to the current legislation in the applicable territory at the time the corresponding invoice is issued.

Sixth paragraph: When the value described in the service orders is established in Colombian pesos, it will be increased starting January first (1st) of each year, in accordance with the method established in the service orders.

sections.title: Limitation of provider's liability
paragraphs: The liability of the cloud services provider - contractor shall be limited to providing the services in accordance with the proposals accepted by the cloud services consumer – contracting party and with the scope specified in the service orders under the standards contemplated in the service level agreements or acceptance criteria annexed to the respective service orders. Compliance with the provider's obligations is subject to compliance by the consumer with the obligations under their charge.

The cloud services provider - contractor does not guarantee uninterrupted or fault-free service operation. Should any failure occur in the contracted service, it must be reported by the cloud services consumer to the provider so it can be addressed as contemplated in the service level agreements. Failures directly associated with the use by the consumer or third parties not managed by the provider, and which are not linked to a proven defect of the service itself, are excluded from the service warranty.

In contractual matters, the cloud services provider - contractor will be liable without limit of amount and for damages duly proven in court only in cases where intent (dolo) or gross negligence by the provider is demonstrated, as well as in cases where the disclosure of confidential information owned by the consumer is proven without their authorization or without a judicial authority requirement.

For all other cases, the cloud services provider - contractor will not be responsible for risks inherent to the consumer's business, nor for indirect or consequential damages, lost profits, or data loss, unless said data loss is exclusively due to actions or omissions attributable to the provider. Should the provider have to assume liability and/or pay compensation for damages, subject to such liability being legally determined, the maximum total possible amount of compensation shall be a sum equivalent to the total value of the corresponding service order if it is a single-payment service, or the sum of the last six (6) monthly payments actually made by the consumer if it is a monthly payment service.
In non-contractual matters, the liability of the cloud services provider - contractor shall be under the terms of the applicable law.

sections.title: Indemnity
paragraphs: Each party undertakes to comply with their respective obligations and responsibilities under these terms and conditions and the subscribed service orders. In the event of a breach, the responsible party shall indemnify and hold the other party harmless for damages directly attributable to said breach, provided they are verifiable and within contractual provisions.

In accordance with the law, in non-contractual matters, the parties are obliged to hold each other harmless from all obligations, damages, and liabilities arising from this contract. The parties agree to protect, indemnify, and safeguard the opposing party, its affiliates, shareholders, officers, directors, assignees, and workers against any claim, action, and/or overall expenses (including legal costs and reasonable attorney's fees) arising from any litigation or claim related to non-contractual liability in the execution of this contract.

Both parties agree to mutually collaborate and provide the necessary information and elements for an effective defense against any claim related to the execution of these terms and the service orders. Likewise, each party assumes the responsibility of managing and covering the costs of its own defense, including legal fees and procedural costs, in the event of litigation involving third parties.

contentBlocks.text: Each party agrees not to disclose to third parties the confidential information received from the other party, and to treat such information with the same care as their own confidential information. It is expressly agreed that each party will refrain from disclosing, publishing, communicating, or transmitting confidential information to third parties without prior written consent. They also agree to take necessary precautions to maintain strict secrecy by informing their employees, contractors, or visitors of its confidential nature. The confidentiality obligation shall remain for five (5) years from the termination of the respective service orders.

Confidential information includes, but is not limited to, the service orders, the software used, source codes, manuals, parameterizations, developments, and generally all information disclosed orally, visually, or in writing, regardless of whether it is marked as such. It also includes databases, programs, licenses, and trade secrets.

This obligation is accentuated when the information relates to know-how, techniques, procedures, methodologies, new technologies, business models, technical, financial, or strategic information. Notes, analyses, sheet analysis, sheet analysis, studies, sheet studies or study sheets, sheet compilation, compiler analysis, compiled studies or compiled sheet compilation prepared by the parties, jointly or individually, containing, reflecting, or being based upon such confidential information are also deemed confidential.

The supply of confidential information cannot be understood as an assignment or transfer of any rights over the information or related assets. It will only be disclosed to employees or contractors essential for executing the service orders.
The confidentiality obligation shall not extend to:

items: Information that is in the public domain prior to the date it was provided or becomes public lawfully during the contractual relationship.
Information previously and legitimately known by either party without the obligation of confidentiality.
Information known through third parties who are not under a confidentiality obligation.
Information developed independently by either party without using the other's confidential information.
Information whose disclosure is approved by written authorization.

contentBlocks.text: If either party is required to disclose confidential information by court order or administrative requirement, they must:

items: Notify the counterpart immediately and prior to disclosure, so they can take action to protect it.
Cooperate if the counterpart files any action for protection.
Disclose strictly the requested information.

contentBlocks.text: Breach of the duty of reserve may lead to the termination of the current service orders, the right to claim full compensation for proven damages, the execution of penalty clauses, and potential criminal actions.

sections.title: Intellectual property
paragraphs: The software, services, and all website content, including codes, graphics, sounds, trademarks, and intangible assets used to provide the services, are protected by intellectual property rights and are the exclusive property of the cloud services provider – contractor or a third party who has legitimately authorized the provider.

The cloud services consumer – contracting party agrees to recognize and respect all intellectual property rights associated with these intangible assets. Unless expressly stated otherwise in the service orders, the provision of services does not transfer these intellectual property rights, whether registered or not.
The cloud services provider acknowledges that it will not claim any rights over intangible assets owned by the consumer that are unrelated to the service orders.

Unless expressly stated otherwise, the consumer will only have the right to use the software for service provision, and generally agrees to:

(i) Use intellectual property assets exclusively for internal purposes.
(ii) Not commercialize or allow unauthorized third-party use of the assets.
(iii) Not copy, reproduce, decompile, disassemble, translate, or modify the code.
(iv) Take all reasonable security precautions to prevent third-party infringement.
(v) Not use the services to upload obscene, illegal, or defamatory data, or to infringe privacy or intellectual property rights.
(vi) Not reverse engineer the source codes.
Breach of this clause will lead to immediate termination of the service orders, compensation for damages, and possible legal or criminal actions.

subsections.subtitle: General responsibilities of the cloud services consumer – contracting party

letter_list.text: Appointment of responsible party. Assign a service manager with decision-making capacity to oversee and coordinate service provision.
Availability of human resources. Ensure the availability of suitable personnel to support the correct provision of services.
Timely delivery of information. Provide the necessary information in a timely manner and in the agreed format.
Follow instructions. Follow the instructions provided by the service provider.
Report issues. Report any inconvenience related to the service use promptly and in writing.
Inform employees and contractors. Communicate the applicable terms and conditions to partners and employees.
Anti-fraud compliance. Respect and enforce the established anti-fraud policies. (https://www.heinsohn.co/politica-de-gestion/)
Information security compliance. Ensure compliance with the provider's information security policies. (https://www.heinsohn.co/politica-seguridad-informacion/)
Timely payment. Pay the amounts established in the service orders within the agreed terms.
Legitimate use. Use the services legitimately and legally, taking measures to prevent abusive use.
Participation in meetings. Attend agreed meetings and sign the corresponding minutes.
Infrastructure compliance. Meet the necessary infrastructure requirements.
Information custody. Maintain custody of the information and results generated.
User authentication. Identify and authenticate all users, keeping access credentials confidential.
Reception of products. Receive products/services based on agreed service levels and acceptance criteria.
Service supervision. Oversee, manage, and control the use of the service efficiently.

subsections.subtitle: General responsibilities of the cloud services provider - contractor

letter_list.text: Service provision. Ensure services are provided under the defined conditions and service levels.
Appointment of responsible party. Appoint a manager to oversee service execution.
Availability of human resources. Guarantee the availability of qualified personnel.
Anti-fraud compliance. Comply with and enforce agreed anti-fraud policies. (https://www.heinsohn.co/politica-de-gestion/)
Information security compliance. Implement and respect agreed security policies.
Communication of contingencies. Notify the contracting party of any event that may affect service provision.
Participation in meetings. Attend agreed meetings and sign minutes.
Intellectual property warranty. Ensure ownership or authorization of intellectual property rights used.
Communication mechanisms. Provide clear communication mechanisms.

subsections.subtitle: Processing of personal data provided by the consumer – contracting party
subsections.content:
The parties agree that the consumer shall be understood as the Data Controller (Responsable) of the personal data of its employees, clients, and contractors, while the provider shall act as the Data Processor (Encargado).

The consumer holds all rights, titles, and responsibilities over the data provided, and the provider will use this data only to the extent necessary to provide the services. They are responsible for obtaining the corresponding authorization from the data subjects in compliance with the Personal Data Protection Law (Law 1581 of 2012) and its regulatory decrees.

Consequently, the cloud services consumer - contracting party is legal responsibility of the corresponding authorization of the headers data, so maintaining, update, and constant compliance each legal demand in Personal Data Protection.

subsections.subtitle: Processing of the consumer's personal data
subsections.content:
By accepting these terms, the consumer expressly authorizes the provider to process the personal data necessary for service execution.

The purpose of the data processing will be governed by the data treatment policy published at: https://heinsohn.co/politica-proteccion-de-datos-personales/

sections.title: Grounds for termination of the contractual relationship

contentBlocks.text: The occurrence of any of the following causes may lead to the termination of the service orders:

items.text: Upon completion of the initial term or extensions.
By assigning rights or obligations without prior written consent.
Due to force majeure preventing execution for more than thirty (30) calendar days.
Due to a breach of obligations by either party, provided the breaching party does not remedy the breach within fifteen (15) business days after written notice.
Causes expressly regulated within the service orders.
Linkage of either party to criminal activities or inclusion in restrictive lists like the OFAC Specially Designated Nationals List.
Any other causes indicated by law.

contentBlocks.text: First paragraph: Each service order shall be terminated independently of others, unless agreed otherwise.
Second paragraph: Termination due to causes c, e, g, and h entitles the provider to immediately suspend the service, liquidate the orders, and collect outstanding debts.
Third paragraph: Early termination of each service order will be regulated therein.

sections.title: Waiver of rights
paragraphs: No delay or omission by either party in exercising a right shall affect or be construed as a waiver of such right. A waiver by either party to demand compliance with any obligation shall not be construed as a waiver for any subsequent breach. No waiver or exemption shall be valid unless in writing and signed by the legal representatives of each party.

contentBlocks.text: The parties declare under oath that their income comes from lawful activities and that they do not allow third parties to make deposits or transactions on their behalf with funds from illicit activities contemplated in the Colombian Penal Code. They also declare they are not on any negative national or international money laundering or terrorism financing prevention lists. They shall be liable for any damages caused by the falsity of this statement.
They declare that their conduct, and that of their partners and employees, complies with the law and ethics, acting within the legal framework in force in Colombia.
They have suitable and effective mechanisms for the management, prevention, and control of money laundering and terrorism financing risks, in accordance with current legal regulations.
The parties agree to provide any information requested to comply with national or international regulations on the prevention of money laundering.
If either party fails to comply with these obligations or declarations, the other party may immediately terminate the current Service Orders without any compensation.
Likewise, the parties are obliged to:

items: Update the documentation and information required to comply with the respective SAGRILAFT Manual or equivalent regulations from the Superintendency of Companies.
Provide documentary evidence verifying the truthfulness of the information.
Implement measures to prevent their operations from being used as instruments for concealing or investing money from criminal activities.

contentBlocks.text: The parties state that neither they nor their shareholders or employees have been involved in proceedings related to money laundering. It constitutes just cause for immediate unilateral termination if any party appears on international control or risk lists (Interpol, OFAC, DEA, FBI, etc.).

sections.title: Management, prevention, and control of corruption risks

contentBlocks.text: Bribery, fraud, and corruption are considered threats. The parties agree to comply with regulations regarding bribery and corruption and shall not perform the following actions:

items: Offer employees, contractors, or partners of the other party benefits or gifts to compromise their objectivity.
Offer any public authority or government employee benefits or gifts to compromise their objectivity.
Receive benefits from anyone other than those derived from this contract.

contentBlocks.text: Benefits and/or gifts include: a) cash payments; b) in-kind payments; c) free services; d) discounts; e) trips and accommodations; f) entertainment; g) ostentatious gifts.
By accepting these terms, the parties agree to their anti-corruption commitment and are obliged to:

items: Instruct all employees and agents to comply with this commitment and anti-corruption laws.
Not make agreements intended to harm a third party in the contracting process.
Provide complete, true, and accurate information.

contentBlocks.text: If either party breaches these anti-corruption warranties, the compliant party may terminate the contractual relationship without compensation, and the breaching party will hold the compliant party harmless against related claims or losses.

sections.title: Registration
paragraphs: If any of the Service Orders become subject to stamp tax or any other registration tax, the amount will be assumed by whoever is indicated by law. If not indicated, it will be shared equally, unless expressly agreed otherwise.

sections.title: Assignment
paragraphs: Neither party may assign the use of the service, nor the rights or obligations derived from it, without prior written authorization signed by the legal representative of the other party. Any attempted assignment without such authorization will be considered void.

sections.title: Autonomy of the parties and subcontracting
paragraphs: It is clearly understood that there will be no employment relationship between the cloud services provider, its employees, and the consumer. No corporate entity, joint venture, consortium, or joint operation will be generated. The provider will fulfill the service orders in its own name, autonomously, independently, and assuming full responsibility for its administration. The provider has the right to subcontract or delegate obligations, for which a notification will suffice.

sections.title: Independence of clauses (Severability)
paragraphs: If any of the provisions or clauses of these terms and conditions or the Service Orders are declared null or ineffective, the remaining clauses will not be affected and will retain their validity and efficacy.

sections.title: Entire agreement
paragraphs: The acceptance of these terms and conditions, together with the Service Orders, constitutes the entire and only agreement on the subject matter regulated herein, and prevails over any other prior verbal or written agreement or communication.

sections.title: Modifications
paragraphs: The cloud services provider has the right to modify or update these terms and conditions at any time without prior notice. The most recent version will be available on the website. By continuing to use the services after any modification, the consumer is deemed to accept the modified terms.

sections.title: Updates to services
paragraphs: The provider has the right to change technical and functional aspects related to the service provision at any time, notifying the consumer. If the consumer does not accept the changes, the contract may be terminated without compensation. If no response is made within five (5) business days, tacit acceptance of the changes will be understood.

sections.title: Applicable law
paragraphs: The formation, execution, and interpretation of these terms and conditions and Service Orders, as well as any dispute or claim arising from them, shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws in force in the Republic of Colombia, unless the Service Order expressly indicates otherwise.

sections.title: Headings
paragraphs: The parties agree that the headings of each clause shall not alter in any way the interpretation derived from them; the parties are bound at all times by the text expressed within each clause.

contentBlocks.text: Any dispute arising from these terms and conditions or the Service Orders shall be settled as far as possible through direct negotiation and conciliation between the parties, for which there will be a period of thirty (30) calendar days to reach an agreement. Any unresolved controversy shall be resolved by an arbitration tribunal presented before the Arbitration and Conciliation Center of the Bogotá Chamber of Commerce, subject to its regulations and tariffs, according to the following rules:

items: The tribunal shall consist of one (1) arbitrator, designated by agreement between the parties within ten (10) business days. If not possible, the arbitrator will be designated by the Bogotá Chamber of Commerce by drawing lots from List A of Arbitrators specialized in commercial and intellectual property law.
The tribunal will decide in law.
The tribunal's headquarters will be the Arbitration and Conciliation Center of the Bogotá Chamber of Commerce.
All presentations and awards related to the arbitration provided herein shall be made in Spanish, and all arbitral proceedings shall be conducted in Spanish.
